_description: THRM-01/02/03: Compression work, fill volumes, and jet recovery accounting
_units: SI throughout: J, m³, Pa, dimensionless

THRM_01_compression_work.note_W_iso: Isothermal lower bound: reversible compressor (theoretical minimum). DO NOT use as W_pump in COP calculations (Pitfall M1).
THRM_01_compression_work.note_W_adia: Adiabatic upper bound: isentropic compressor. W_pump = W_adia / eta_c.
THRM_01_compression_work.COP_ideal_max_note: COP_ideal_max = W_iso / W_pump is the ideal ceiling COP if ALL buoyancy work were recovered with no losses. It is below 1.0 for all eta_c, confirming buoyancy alone cannot achieve COP > 1.0. The target COP = 1.5 requires hydrofoil contribution (Phase 2+).

THRM_02_fill_volumes.fill_condition: Air injected at P_bottom = 280352.6 Pa (= 2.76686 atm) occupies V_depth = 0.072356 m³ = 2.5550 ft³. As vessel ascends, air expands isothermally per Boyle's law: V(z) = V_surface * P_atm / P(z). At z=H: V(H) = V_surface * P_atm / P_atm = V_surface = 0.2002 m³ — vessel is exactly full. Fill target: inject V_depth = 0.072356 m³ of air at P_bottom into open-bottom vessel. NOTE: CONVENTIONS.md displays V_depth = 0.07228 m³ (rounded); precise = 0.072356 m³.

THRM_03_jet_recovery.explanation: W_jet = 0 as a separate line item in the energy balance. Jet recovery from the expanding open-bottom air column during ascent is thermodynamically identical to the buoyancy expansion work — it is NOT an additional energy source. The buoyancy integral W_buoy = integral_0^H F_b(z) dz already accounts for ALL work done by the buoyant/expanding gas column. The buoyancy force F_b(z) = rho_w * g * V(z) includes the full volume expansion at each height, which encodes the gas expansion pressure effect. Adding W_jet separately would double-count Pitfall C6. Therefore: W_jet is NOT an additional energy source; it is the same thermodynamic expansion already included in W_buoy.
THRM_03_jet_recovery.pitfall_guarded: C6 (double-counting jet recovery with buoyancy work)
